title: Tighten the middle act
description: Remove repeated beats and enter scenes later to restore forward movement.
solves: A second act that loses momentum.

title: Preserve long takes
description: Let performance and blocking carry complete dramatic passages without coverage-driven cutting.
solves: A cut that breaks performance continuity and spatial tension.

title: Montage acceleration
description: Compress process and elapsed time into a rhythmic progression of images.
solves: Necessary story development that consumes too much runtime.

title: Nonlinear restructure
description: Reorder story information so cause, memory, and consequence create a new dramatic pattern.
solves: A predictable structure that reveals its meaning too early.

title: Remove exposition
description: Cut dialogue and beats that explain information already carried by action or image.
solves: Scenes that repeat meaning and underestimate the audience.

title: Add reaction shots
description: Use available coverage to hold on characters as information lands.
solves: Plot turns that register intellectually but not emotionally.

title: Crosscut the climax
description: Interleave simultaneous actions to build convergence, urgency, and dramatic irony.
solves: A climax with strong material but limited escalation.

title: Slow cinema patience
description: Protect duration, silence, and observation so the viewer experiences time with the characters.
solves: A film whose identity depends on contemplation rather than plot velocity.
